chapter_title.en: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes

text: 5345 - وَ رَوَى وُهَيْبُ بْنُ حَفْصٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ غُلاَمٍ دَخَلَ دَارَ قَوْمٍ يَلْعَبُ فَوَقَعَ فِي بِئْرِهِمْ أَ يَضْمَنُونَ قَالَ "لَيْسَ يَضْمَنُونَ وَ إِنْ كَانُوا مُتَّهَمِينَ ضَمِنُوا".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5345 - Wuhayb ibn Hafs narrated from Abu Basir, who narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: I asked Imam (as) about a boy who entered the house of some people to play and fell into their well. Are they liable?
Imam (as) said: "They are not liable, but if they are accused (of negligence or wrongdoing), then they are liable."
